Vigna unguiculata, commonly known as Cowpea or Black-eyed pea, is a plant species native to Africa and Asia, known for its edible beans.

The seeds of Vigna unguiculata contain various bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

The health benefits of Vigna unguiculata include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Vigna unguiculata include tea, powder, and capsule, which are used to alleviate various health conditions, including hypertension, diabetes, and digestive issues.

properties: Vignea conjuncta, commonly known as the wild yam, has been used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation. It is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of infections. Additionally, Vignea conjuncta may have estrogenic properties, which can help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of this plant.
